providers.name: Twilio
providers.rate_limits: 100 concurrent requests for the REST API. SMS throughput is governed by the sender type: 1 segment per second (MPS) for long codes and up to 100 MPS for short codes.
providers.auth_notes: Uses HTTP Basic Authentication. The 'Account SID' acts as the username and the 'Auth Token' acts as the password. Credentials must be sent in the Authorization header.

providers.name: MessageBird
providers.rate_limits: 500 requests per second (RPS) for POST (write) operations and 50 RPS for GET, PATCH, and DELETE operations.
providers.auth_notes: Uses an API Access Key. The key must be included in the HTTP Authorization header in the format: 'Authorization: AccessKey {your_access_key}'.

providers.name: Vonage (formerly Nexmo)
providers.rate_limits: Default account limit is 30 requests per second for the SMS API. Note that US outbound SMS is restricted by carriers to 1 request per second per long code.
providers.auth_notes: Supports multiple methods: API Key and Secret passed as query parameters (api_key, api_secret) or via Basic Auth header. Newer APIs also support JSON Web Tokens (JWT).

providers.name: Plivo
providers.rate_limits: 300 API requests per 5 seconds for general API calls. Outbound SMS delivery is throttled at 5 messages per second per account by default.
providers.auth_notes: Uses Basic Authentication requiring an 'Auth ID' and 'Auth Token'. These are found in the Plivo console and must be provided with every API request.

effort_recommendation.rationale: No native node - requires HTTP Request configuration
